snapshot: CME Nikkei 225 June futures were 53,670 at 23:23 JST, down 905 versus the prior close, so index tone is soft into Friday's open.
Brent moved back above $100 and WTI traded around $94. That helps upstream energy, while transport and broad cyclicals stay exposed.
USD/JPY was around 159 at Tokyo 15:00 after touching 159.24, keeping both exporter support and intervention risk on the table.
Reuters' Europe-morning note had S&P 500 and Nasdaq futures each down 1%. U.S. cash trading is still open at publication, so this is a flash note rather than a full close-based read.

drivers.driver: Oil shock, a weaker yen and softer index futures
drivers.why_today: CME Nikkei futures at 53,670, Brent back above $100 and USD/JPY near 159 point to a weaker index open but relative support for upstream energy.

drivers.driver: An unusually strong after-close disclosure slate
drivers.why_today: Kyoto FG on shareholder returns, Ridge-i on a large AI order plus a guidance raise, and Timee on an outlook raise give stock-specific setups a chance to beat the tape.

headline_inventory.headline: CME Nikkei 225 futures were 53,670 at 23:23 JST, down 905 from the prior close
USD/JPY was around 159 at Tokyo 15:00 after briefly touching 159.24
Brent moved back above $100 while S&P 500 and Nasdaq futures were each down 1%
Kyoto FG disclosed a larger buyback authorization and a longer buyback window
Ridge-i disclosed a 410 million yen AI project order from SBI Group
Timee raised its FY ending April 2026 outlook
Honda swung to a full-year loss outlook after canceling three EV models
U.S. stocks fell about 1% on Middle East tensions and private-credit worries

watchlist.name: Kyoto Financial Group
watchlist.why_today: It raised FY guidance on Nintendo share-sale gains and paired that with a dividend increase and a larger buyback. The disclosure quality is high enough to outrun weak index tone.
watchpoints: Can it hold the 4,300 yen area after the opening print
Does volume stay alive after the first burst
Does it outperform the regional-bank group rather than simply follow it
falsifiers: The market focuses only on the one-off nature of the share-sale gain
Thursday's rally already priced in the stronger shareholder returns
watchlist.red_team: The earnings jump is driven more by share-sale gains than by core operations, so the initial re-rating can fade.

sources.title: 京都ＦＧ(5844) 自己株式の取得枠拡大および取得期間延長に関するお知らせ
京都ＦＧ---大幅続伸、大幅な増配や自社株買いの上限引き上げを好感

watchlist.name: Ridge-i
watchlist.why_today: A FY guidance raise and a 410 million yen AI order from SBI Group hit at the same time. Both theme appeal and numeric support are there.
watchpoints: Does the market read the 410 million yen order as the start of a wider pipeline rather than a one-off
Can it absorb profit-taking after a likely gap-up
Does SBI-related cross-sell speculation broaden
falsifiers: Small-cap growth profit-taking takes over immediately
Investors question the repeatability of the order flow
watchlist.red_team: The disclosure is strong, but Growth-market names can turn into pure volatility trades very quickly.

sources.title: リッジアイ(5572) 資本業務提携先であるSBIグループからのAI関連プロジェクト大型受注のお知らせ
リッジアイ(5572) 2026年７月期連結業績予想の修正に関するお知らせ

watchlist.name: Timee
watchlist.why_today: It lifted its FY ending April 2026 range after strong first-quarter progress. For growth, this is one of the cleaner number-backed releases.
watchpoints: Does the market keep rewarding the first-quarter margin improvement after the open
Does the upper end of the revised range become the anchor
Can it outperform a weak broader growth tape
falsifiers: Short-term money exits after the event passes
Investors lean too hard on the wide guidance range
watchlist.red_team: Even good growth earnings can fail to travel if the wider growth board opens heavy.

sources.title: タイミー、今期経常を37億～40億円に上方修正
タイミー(215A) 2026年4月期 第1四半期決算説明資料

watchlist.name: INPEX
watchlist.why_today: Brent is back above $100 and USD/JPY is near 159. In a weak index tape, upstream energy remains a relative-strength candidate.
watchpoints: Does the stock keep tracking oil higher after the open
Can it stay relatively firm against broader index selling
Does the weaker-yen tailwind remain intact
falsifiers: Oil reverses sharply lower on reserve-release hopes
A broader market drop drags resource shares down with everything else
watchlist.red_team: This is a headline-driven commodity trade, so one geopolitical swing can flip the setup quickly.

sources.title: Dollar nears 2026 highs as volatile oil fans inflation spike fears
今日の株式見通し＝軟調、原油高に反応

watchlist.name: Recruit Holdings
watchlist.why_today: It disclosed cancellation of treasury stock equal to 5.84% of shares outstanding. That makes it a liquid supply-improvement name even in shaky index conditions.
watchpoints: Can it stay relatively firm even if the index opens weak
Does the market re-rate the size of the cancellation
Can the capital-action angle beat wider growth selling
falsifiers: Nasdaq weakness spills over into online-service and talent names
The cancellation is treated as already priced in
watchlist.red_team: Because this is a mega-cap, the catalyst may improve supply but not necessarily force an immediate gap-and-go move.

sources.title: リクルート(6098) 自己株式の消却について
本日の〖自社株買い〗銘柄 (11日大引け後 発表分)

watchlist.name: JAPEX
watchlist.why_today: WTI around $94 and Brent above $100 keep the secondary resource-chain trade alive behind INPEX.
watchpoints: Does money rotate from INPEX into secondary oil and gas exposure
Does volume expand after the opening print
Does gas exposure add to the simple oil trade
falsifiers: Capital stays concentrated in INPEX as the primary beneficiary
Oil loses momentum and the sympathy bid stops
watchlist.red_team: This is more of a secondary beneficiary than a pure first-order winner, so the trade can lose energy quickly.

sources.title: Dollar nears 2026 highs as volatile oil fans inflation spike fears
今日の株式見通し＝軟調、原油高に反応

watchlist.name: Aida Engineering
watchlist.why_today: It announced cancellation of 4.3 million treasury shares, equal to 6.72% of shares outstanding. That's a meaningful capital-policy signal.
watchpoints: Does the market reward the cancellation ratio cleanly at the open
Can it absorb weakness in machinery peers
Does buying re-emerge after the first round of selling
falsifiers: The broad tape overwhelms the capital-action story
Investors see the cancellation as largely pre-telegraphed
watchlist.red_team: The capital signal is clear, but without a parallel earnings angle, follow-through buying can be limited.

sources.title: アイダ(6118) 自己株式の消却に関するお知らせ
本日の〖自社株買い〗銘柄 (12日大引け後 発表分)

watchlist.name: Bestera
watchlist.why_today: It paired an earnings beat versus guidance with an AI implementation project alongside HEROZ. The existing niche story now has an AI overlay as well.
watchpoints: Which part of the message leads at the open: earnings upside or the AI angle
Can it hold any opening gap rather than instantly fade
Does the move come with real turnover
falsifiers: The AI collaboration is treated as a short-lived theme headline
Small-cap profit-taking turns it into a gap-and-fade
watchlist.red_team: The messages are somewhat split, so the market may struggle to decide which part deserves the premium.

deep_dive.title: Kyoto FG: One-off gains or not, the open should test the strength of the return reset

lines: This is less a core-earnings story than a capital-return re-rating story, and that still matters into the open.
Among regional banks, liquidity is sufficient for the market to express the view quickly.
After the March 11 close, Kyoto FG disclosed a guidance raise, a bigger year-end dividend and an expanded buyback.
The stock already made a new high intraday on March 12, so Friday's open is about whether follow-through survives.
Can it hold the 4,300 yen area after the opening print instead of filling the gap immediately.
Does volume remain active after the first 30 minutes, and does it keep outperforming the broader regional-bank group.
If investors reduce the story to a one-off profit uplift from Nintendo share sales only.
If the dividend and buyback changes were already largely priced in on Thursday.
March 13 is major SQ, so index-driven flow can overwhelm stock-specific logic.
If the market de-risks further, even stronger shareholder returns may not protect the name intraday.

no_trade_conditions: If March 13 major-SQ index flow overwhelms stock-specific catalysts
If CME Nikkei futures reverse sharply from 53,670, or break lower enough to invalidate the setup before the Japan open
If Brent or WTI reverse lower abruptly and the relative-energy bid disappears
If USD/JPY snaps back on intervention fears and the weaker-yen premise breaks
